display.purpose: Hero name, primary identity. Fraunces with optical size tuning.

headline.purpose: Section titles, case study names. Large, confident serif.

body.purpose: Running text, max 30-34ch line length. Warm sans, comfortable reading.

label.purpose: Tabs, form labels, footer text. Small, often uppercase.

mono.purpose: Hidden words, machines tab, code. Precise, quiet.

motion.name: ease
motion.purpose: The only easing curve on the site. Used everywhere.

motion.name: dur-micro
motion.purpose: Hover states, color swaps, instant feedback.

motion.name: dur-large
motion.purpose: Section transitions, theme shifts, reveal animations.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Hero grid switches from 2-col to 1-col; tab layout changes.

components.name: Plain Text Link
components.description: No chrome, color-based affordance. Accent-rose on hover.
components.css: .ds-link { color: hsl(340 10% 40%); text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.6; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-link:hover { color: hsl(343 42% 46%); }

components.name: Plain Text Tab
components.description: Horizontal tab group, no pill. Active tab is accent-colored.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: flex; gap: 1.2rem; flex-wrap: wrap; margin-bottom: 1.5rem; } .ds-tabs button { font-size: 0.78rem; color: hsl(340 10% 40%); background: none; border: 0; padding: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-tabs button:hover { color: hsl(340 30% 16%); } .ds-tabs button[aria-selected="true"] { color: hsl(343 42% 46%); }

components.name: Work Card
components.description: 16/10 cover with tinted blend background. Lifts on hover.
components.html:
Mobility at scale
Growing adoption in Angola
components.css: .ds-card { display: block; text-decoration: none; color: hsl(340 30% 16%); } .ds-card-cover { aspect-ratio: 16 / 10; border-radius: 12px; background: color-mix(in oklab, hsl(343 42% 46%) 30%, hsl(345 30% 92%)); transition: transform 420ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1), background-color 750ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } @media (hover: hover) { .ds-card:hover .ds-card-cover { transform: translateY(-4px); } .ds-card:hover .ds-card-title { color: hsl(343 42% 46%); } } .ds-card-title { margin-top: 0.8rem; font-family: '"Fraunces Variable", Georgia, serif'; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.005em; font-size: 0.95rem; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-card-tagline { margin-top: 0.25rem; font-size: 0.78rem; color: hsl(340 10% 40%); line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Interactive Phrase (Hover Reveal)
components.description: Accent text that blooms with soft background on hover. Reveals hidden text below.
components.css: .ds-phrase { display: inline; font: inherit; letter-spacing: inherit; color: hsl(343 42% 46%); background-color: transparent; border: 0; padding: 0.08em 0.04em; border-radius: 0.22em; cursor: pointer; box-decoration-break: clone; transition: background-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-phrase:hover { background-color: color-mix(in oklab, hsl(343 35% 88%) 75%, transparent); }

components.name: Hero Name & Role
components.description: Large display name in accent color, small muted role below.
components.css: .ds-hero-id { text-align: right; } .ds-hero-name { font-family: '"Fraunces Variable", Georgia, serif'; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.005em; font-size: clamp(1.05rem, 1.6vw, 1.35rem); line-height: 1.1; color: hsl(343 42% 46%); } .ds-hero-role { margin-top: 0.4rem; color: hsl(340 10% 40%); font-size: 0.78rem; }

components.name: Footer Link
components.description: Small text link in footer. Muted by default, accent on hover.
components.css: .ds-footer-link { color: hsl(340 10% 40%); text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.75rem; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1); } .ds-footer-link:hover { color: hsl(343 42% 46%); }

narrative.overview: This is the workspace of someone meticulous—where every detail is intentional and nothing is accidental. The design is a quiet collaborator: typography and color carry the narrative, motion is purposeful, and components are clean without chrome. The portfolio speaks through the work itself, not through decoration or showiness. Playfulness lives in the interactions, not the surface. Hover states, hidden reveals, and a theme system that invites exploration make the experience feel alive—but always in service of clarity, never at its expense.
keyCharacteristics: Serif display + sans body typography hierarchy
Purple progression with six carefully hand-tuned theme stops
Intentional motion with respect for reduced-motion preferences
Playful details (hidden words, cursor veil, hover reveals)

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: The primary accent appears on ≤10% of any screen. Its rarity is the point. Overuse drains it of power.

rules.name: The Theme Interpolation Rule
rules.body: All colors interpolate between theme stops via the theme engine. The frontend writes resolved theme values to CSS custom properties at runtime; nothing is baked.

rules.name: The Weight Consistency Rule
rules.body: Serif faces use weight 600 (Fraunces). Sans and mono remain at their default (Inter 400). No weight mixing within a family.

rules.name: The Baseline Rule
rules.body: All text aligns to a shared baseline grid (line-height 1.6 on body, 1.15 on display). This creates invisible scaffolding.

rules.name: The No-Overflow Rule
rules.body: Headings with large clamp() max values must be tested at every breakpoint. If text overflows its container, reduce the clamp max or rewrite the copy. The viewport is part of the design.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: No drop shadows on elements. Depth comes from tonal shifts and motion, not blur. The goal is clarity; shadow vocabulary clutters the interface.

rules.name: The Hover-Lift Rule
rules.body: Interactive elements (cards, phrases) respond to hover with a subtle translateY lift plus a color shift. No box-shadow; motion + color = feedback.

dos: Do use Fraunces 600 for all headlines and display text. It conveys craft and authority.
Do keep accent color to ≤10% of any screen. Its rarity makes it powerful.
Do use muted gray for secondary copy; it reads as confident, not timid. Ensure 4.5:1 contrast.
Do animate via opacity and transform only (translate, scale, rotate). Never animate layout.
Do respect reduced-motion: provide opacity-only crossfades as the alternative to transforms.
Do use one easing curve everywhere: cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1). No bounce, no elastic.
Do use three duration tiers: micro (200ms), small (420ms), large (750ms).
Do test all clamp() values at every breakpoint to catch heading overflow.
Do let the work speak. Components should be invisible; content should shine.
Do provide hover feedback on interactive elements. Subtle, never gratuitous.
donts: Don't use shadows for depth. Tonal shifts and motion convey elevation.
Don't add motion for motion's sake. If there's no meaning to the motion, remove it.
Don't use bright, neon, or saturated accent colors. Rose, lavender, and plum are muted intentionally.
Don't introduce new serif or sans families. Stick to Fraunces (display) and Inter (body).
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left / border-right > 1px as colored accents). Never intentional.
Don't create gradient text (background-clip: text). It's decorative and reduces readability.
Don't use nested cards. Tonal shifts are enough; stacked chrome clutters the page.
Don't render interactive elements with no hover state. Every clickable thing should respond.
Don't let the design compete with the work. The portfolio is about outcomes, not visual effects.
Don't feel pressured to fill silence with decoration. Whitespace is a design tool here.
Don't vague or hide the actual outcomes. Show impact directly: scale, metrics, user outcomes.
